channel.title: Politics on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Politics on The Daily Thrum

item.title: Progressive Presenter Releases Book On Pretending Not To Understand Things In Order To Humiliate Callers
item.description:
LONDON — A progressive-liberal radio presenter renowned for spending twelve minutes pretending not to understand a caller’s perfectly clear point has released a new book teaching aspiring broadcasters how to convert political disagreement into a procedural competency test.

“But In Your Previous Breath You Said Something Else, So It’s Not That, Then, Is It?”, subtitled A Practical Guide to Mogging Right-Wingers offers readers a practical guide to interrupting callers, demanding increasingly specific definitions, ignoring the obvious substance of what they are saying and then declaring victory when they become flustered.

item.title: Podcaster Furious After Followers Start Tagging Him With “Owning Nothing And Being Happy Doesn’t Sound That Bad, Really”
item.description:
LONDON — British podcaster Julian May has reportedly entered a state of ideological emergency after dozens of users began tagging him on X with the phrase, “Owning nothing and being happy doesn’t sound that bad, really.”

May, host of the popular doom podcast Mayday, has spent years warning viewers that global elites intend to strip ordinary people of property, autonomy, privacy, national identity and the spiritual inheritance of Western civilisation before instructing them to “own nothing and be happy”.
